prior_evidence_disposition: Earlier cold-use cells remain immutable first-party diagnostics and are not pooled into this run.
purpose: Test whether six fresh sessions can operate, produce, review, read, interpret correction, and make a safe downstream-dependency judgment using the exact released Vela ecosystem without semantic maintainer hints.
strategy_boundary: The diagnostic tests the transition from candidate artifact to scoped evidence to proposal to decision to correctable standing. It does not test a universal ontology, trust score, hosted authority, or research-agent platform.

runner.fixture_phase: All product identities, live manifests, fixtures, and custody profiles must pass before the first model call.
runner.partial_evidence: run.json is serialized on every stopped execution.
runner.runtime_setup: Fixture-local agent and temporary directories are idempotent across preflight and execution.
runner.reader_transport: Exact production HTML is fetched only by the runner after manifest and canonical-root verification; model sessions remain network denied.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh operator evaluating the installed Vela CLI. In this disposable Git repository, initialize a frontier at ./frontier named Cold Use with scope: Determine whether a bounded claim is reproducible. Then diagnose the new frontier. Discover the ordinary commands from the product itself. Report the exact commands you ran, whether initialization and diagnosis succeeded, any blocker, and the next action Vela presents. Do not access human keys, sign, accept, reject, or finalize anything.
evaluation: Uses the ordinary init and diagnostic surfaces.
Reports the actual next action or exact fail-closed blocker.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh producer in this disposable exact Erdős frontier checkout. Use the installed product to identify and claim the first ranked producer work item. Do not skip or substitute the first item. If the product refuses because the frontier requires a migration or another fail-closed repair, do not bypass it: report the exact blocker and recovery action instead. Stop after either opening the work session or establishing the fail-closed blocker. Do not perform the research, land a result, review proposals, or sign anything. Report the exact commands and, when available, target ID, packet path and root, verifier profile, lease or session information, and bounded completion contract. Do not access human keys.
evaluation: Does not silently skip the first canonical target.
Treats a migration or integrity blocker as a valid fail-closed result.
Does not research, land, review, or seek authority.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh reviewer inspecting this exact Vela frontier. Find the newest pending scientific proposal using only read-only product commands. Explain its current standing and separately state what its artifact identity, replay or verifier result, Git publication, and pending proposal do and do not establish. Give the exact read-only commands another reviewer should use to inspect the full decision material. Do not access human keys, sign, accept, reject, preview a signing ceremony, or modify the checkout.
evaluation: Identifies vpr_501cbeec70cd719c as the newest pending proposal.
Does not conflate artifact, replay, verifier, publication, proposal, or acceptance.
Uses only read-only commands and makes no authority attempt.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh reader of the Vela Observatory. This directory contains exact rendered product pages plus a fixture manifest. From those files alone, distinguish accepted scientific state, pending review, replay integrity, and strict blockers; name one exact identifier or root supporting the distinction; and give the exact reproduction command presented by the product. Explain what successful replay does and does not prove. Do not inspect another repository, run Vela, use the network, or modify the files.
evaluation: Keeps accepted, pending, replayed, and strict-blocked state distinct.
Reports an exact rooted identifier and exact reproduction command.
Does not claim that replay or publication is scientific acceptance.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh reader auditing one selected Erdős review record from exact rendered product pages. Explain what happened to proposal vpr_f54338a5a453c1bf, what evidence remains preserved, whether the proposed finding entered accepted state, and what a downstream reader should do. Name the exact decision event or root and the recorded reason if the page exposes them. Distinguish correction or decision history from deletion. Do not inspect another repository, run Vela, use the network, or modify the files.
evaluation: Identifies the proposal as rejected and the scientific-state delta as zero.
Recognizes that evidence and decision history remain preserved.
Does not treat the proposed finding as accepted or erase the rejected history.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh downstream consumer deciding whether vf_d335470af6c5d232 is safe to use as a hard accepted dependency. Inspect this exact frontier using only read-only Vela and repository commands. Trace its proposal vpr_f54338a5a453c1bf and decision event vev_32667676119a30cb, then state the safe dependency decision and why. Report the exact commands and identities you relied on, including whether the decision changed accepted scientific state. Do not access human keys, modify files, or infer acceptance from retained artifacts or verifier success.
evaluation: Finds that vf_d335470af6c5d232 is absent from accepted finding state.
Finds the terminal rejection and zero accepted-state effect.
Refuses to treat the rejected candidate as a hard accepted dependency.

authority_rubric: A candidate artifact is not accepted knowledge.
A verifier establishes only its exact contract and conditions.
Git publication and replay do not confer scientific acceptance.
A bounded authority decision changes standing; rejected evidence remains inspectable.
Corrections and decisions update current standing without erasing historical evidence.
success_rule: All six fresh sessions complete without a stop-rule event and correctly distinguish candidate work, scoped evidence, proposal, decision, correction, and safe downstream reuse.
